eData validation

Validation functions for eData

Source: vignettes/validation.qmd 
library(eDataDRF)
library(pointblank)
library(gt)

The pointblank package for R provides robust, efficient validation features for data, allowing us to quickly check that our data conforms to the expected format. Although used of controlled vocabulary and the eData app allow us to constrain user-entered data, it is more practical to add an additional level of validation. This programmatic validation layer can be re-used wherever needed; this is particularly valuable when data may need to be transformed as part of the analysis pathway.

Below we demonstrate the use of the validation functions for each table type. Note: The Styling of included tables is currently rather broken, making them difficult to interpret. I will fix this when possible, but as it’s not a functional issue I haven’t prioritised it.

Todo:

  • Add and harmonise example data
  • Test example data runs without error
  • Test validation functions against example data
  • One-line explanation of agent vs pipeline mode

Campaign data validation

Typically when campaign data are generated/extracted for a data object the resulting table will only have one row. However, in downstream operations across multiple data objects, each campaign must have a unique identifier; this is checked as part of the validation below.

example_campaign_tibble() |>
  pb_validate_campaign()
Pointblank Validation
eData Campaign Validation

tibble data
STEP COLUMNS VALUES TBL EVAL UNITS PASS FAIL W S N EXT

1

Check CAMPAIGN_NAME_SHORT and CAMPAIGN_NAME are not blank

col_vals_not_null()

▮CAMPAIGN_NAME_SHORT

 — ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

2

Check CAMPAIGN_NAME_SHORT and CAMPAIGN_NAME are not blank

col_vals_not_null()

▮CAMPAIGN_NAME

 — ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

3

Check each CAMPAIGN_NAME_SHORT and CAMPAIGN_NAME is unique

rows_distinct()

▮CAMPAIGN_NAME_SHORT, ▮CAMPAIGN_NAME

 — ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

4

Check CAMPAIGN_START_DATE is between 01 Jan 1900 and the present date

col_vals_between()

▮CAMPAIGN_START_DATE

[1900-01-01, 2026-03-16]

 ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

5

Check ENTERED_BY and ENTERED_DATE are not blank

col_vals_not_null()

▮ENTERED_BY

 — ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

6

Check ENTERED_BY and ENTERED_DATE are not blank

col_vals_not_null()

▮ENTERED_DATE

 — ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

7

Check ENTERED_DATE is not in the future

col_vals_lte()

▮ENTERED_DATE

2026-03-16

 ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —
2026-03-16 09:19:04 UTC < 1 s 2026-03-16 09:19:04 UTC

References data validation 

example_references_tibble() |>
  pb_validate_reference()
Pointblank Validation
eData Reference Validation

tibble data
STEP COLUMNS VALUES TBL EVAL UNITS PASS FAIL W S N EXT

1

REFERENCE_ID is in the format YearLastnameFirstThreeWords

col_vals_regex()

▮REFERENCE_ID

^\d{4}[A-Za-z0-9]{1,10}([A-Z][a-z]*){1,3}$

 ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

2

REFERENCE_TYPE is in reference_type_vocabulary()

col_vals_in_set()

▮REFERENCE_TYPE

Journal Article, Book, Report, Dataset/Database

 ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

3

DATA_SOURCE is in data_source_vocabulary()

col_vals_in_set()

▮DATA_SOURCE

Primary, Secondary/Review, Other

 ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

4

Check AUTHOR, TITLE are not blank

col_vals_not_null()

▮AUTHOR

 — ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

5

Check AUTHOR, TITLE are not blank

col_vals_not_null()

▮TITLE

 — ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

6

Check YEAR is between 1900 and the current year

col_vals_between()

▮YEAR

[1,900, 2,026]

 ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

7

Check ACCESS_DATE is between 01 Jan 2000 and the present date

col_vals_between()

▮ACCESS_DATE

[2000-01-01, 2026-03-16]

 ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

8

Check PERIODICAL_JOURNAL isn't blank when REFERENCE_TYPE is 'Journal Article'

col_vals_not_null()

▮PERIODICAL_JOURNAL

 — ✓ 1 1
1 		0
0 		— — — —

9

Check PUBLISHER isn't blank when REFERENCE_TYPE is 'Book' or 'Report'

col_vals_not_null()

▮PUBLISHER

 — ✓ 0 0
NA 		0
NA 		— — — —

10

Check INSTITUTION isn't blank when REFERENCE_TYPE is 'Report'

col_vals_not_null()

▮INSTITUTION

 — ✓ 0 0
NA 		0
NA 		— — — —
2026-03-16 09:19:05 UTC < 1 s 2026-03-16 09:19:05 UTC

Sites data validation 

example_sites_tibble() |>
  pb_validate_sites()
Pointblank Validation
eData Sites Validation

tibble data
STEP COLUMNS VALUES TBL EVAL UNITS PASS FAIL W S N EXT

1

Check SITE_CODE and SITE_NAME are not blank

col_vals_not_null()

▮SITE_CODE

 — ✓ 2 2
1 		0
0 		— — — —

2

Check SITE_CODE and SITE_NAME are not blank

col_vals_not_null()

▮SITE_NAME

 — ✓ 2 2
1 		0
0 		— — — —

3

Check no duplicate site code/name combinations

rows_distinct()

▮SITE_CODE, ▮SITE_NAME

 — ✓ 2 2
1 		0
0 		— — — —

Check SITE_GEOGRAPHIC_FEATURE is in geographic_features_vocabulary()

col_vals_in_set()

▮SITE_GEOGRAPHIC_FEATURE

Not relevant, Not reported, River, stream, canal, Lake, pond, pool, reservoir, Ocean, sea, territorial waters, Coastal, fjord, Estuary, Drainage, sewer, artificial water, Swamp, wetland, Groundwater, aquifer, WWTP, Artificial Land/Urban Areas, Landfills, Cropland, Woodland, forest, Shrubland, Grassland, Bare land and lichen/moss, Glacier, Other

1 		0
0 		— — — —

col_vals_in_set()

▮SITE_GEOGRAPHIC_FEATURE_SUB

Not relevant, Not reported, Water surface, Water column, pelagic zone, Water benthos, Other
